revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''宗像神社'''は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。 主な御祭神は...'
revision.text: '''宗像神社'''は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。 主な御祭神は田心姫神・ 湍津姫神・市杵島姫神である。

'''宗像神社'''は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
主な御祭神は田心姫神・ 湍津姫神・市杵島姫神である。
== 全国の宗像神社 == * [[京都御苑内鎮座 宗像神社]]（京都市上京区）

'''宗像神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
主な御祭神は田心姫神・ 湍津姫神・市杵島姫神である。
== 全国の宗像神社 == * [[京都御苑]]内 [[宗像神社 (京都市上京区)|宗像神社]]（京都市上京区）
[[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|むなかたしんしや]]

'''宗像神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
主な御祭神は田心姫神・ 湍津姫神・市杵島姫神である。
== 全国の宗像神社 == * [[京都御苑]]内 [[宗像神社 (京都市上京区)|宗像神社]]（京都市上京区）
[[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|むなかたしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''宗像神社'''('''宗形神社'''、'''胸肩神社'''、'''胸形神社'''などとも)（むなかたじんじゃ）は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。
== 概要 == 宗像三女神([[田心姫神]]・ [[湍津姫神]]・[[市杵島姫神]])を祭神としており、全国各地に同名の神社が存在している。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
[[航海]]の安全を祈願する神社で、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[近畿地方]]の海沿いの地域に多く存在する。
なお、宗形系の神社は、全国で5番目に多いとされている。
== 全国の宗像神社 == *[[京都府]] **[[宗像神社 (京都市上京区)|宗像神社]](京都市上京区、[[京都御苑]]内) *[[岡山県]] **[[宗形神社 (岡山市大窪)|宗形神社]]([[岡山市]]大窪) **[[宗形神社 (岡山市郡)|宗形神社]]([[岡山市]]郡) **[[宗形神社 (赤磐市)|宗形神社]]([[赤磐市]])
== 関連項目 == *[[宗像大社]] *[[宗像三女神]] [[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|むなかたしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''宗像神社'''('''宗形神社'''、'''胸肩神社'''、'''胸形神社'''などとも)（むなかたじんじゃ）は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。
== 概要 == 宗像三女神([[田心姫神]]・ [[湍津姫神]]・[[市杵島姫神]])を祭神としており、全国各地に同名の神社が存在している。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
[[航海]]の安全を祈願する神社で、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[近畿地方]]の海沿いの地域に多く存在する。
なお、宗形系の神社は、全国で5番目に多いとされている。
== 全国の宗像神社 == *[[栃木県]] **[[胸形神社 (小山市)]] *[[京都府]] **[[宗像神社 (京都市上京区)|宗像神社]](京都市上京区、[[京都御苑]]内) *[[岡山県]] **[[宗形神社 (岡山市大窪)|宗形神社]]([[岡山市]]大窪) **[[宗形神社 (岡山市郡)|宗形神社]]([[岡山市]]郡) **[[宗形神社 (赤磐市)|宗形神社]]([[赤磐市]])
== 関連項目 == *[[宗像大社]] *[[宗像三女神]] [[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|むなかたしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

'''宗像神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。'''宗形神社'''、'''胸形神社'''、'''胸肩神社'''などとも表記される。
== 概要 == 宗像三女神（[[タキリビメ|田心姫神]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫神]]）を祭神としており、全国各地に同名の神社が存在している。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
[[航海]]の安全を祈願する神社で、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[近畿地方]]の海沿いの地域に多く存在する。
なお、宗像系の神社は、全国で5番目に多いとされている。

'''宗像神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、'''道主貴'''である[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。'''宗形神社'''、'''胸形神社'''、'''胸肩神社'''などとも表記される。
== 概要 == 宗像三女神（[[タキリビメ|田心姫神]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫神]]）を祭神としており、全国各地に同名の神社が存在している。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
[[航海]]の安全を祈願する神社で、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[近畿地方]]の海沿いの地域に多く存在する。関東地区の[[印旛沼]]周辺には宗像神社が集中する地域がある。
なお、宗像系の神社は、全国で5番目に多いとされている。

== 概要 == 宗像三女神（[[タキリビメ|田心姫神]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫神]]）を祭神としており、全国各地に同名の神社が存在している。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
[[航海]]の安全を祈願する神社で、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[近畿地方]]の海沿いの地域に多く存在する。関東地区の[[印旛沼]]周辺には宗像神社が集中する地域がある。
なお、宗像系の神社は、全国で5番目に多いとされている。

'''宗像神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、'''道主貴'''である[[宗像三女神]]を祀る神社である。'''宗形神社'''、'''胸形神社'''、'''胸肩神社'''などとも表記される。
== 概要 == 宗像三女神（[[タキリビメ|田心姫神]]、[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫神]]、[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫神]]）を祭神としており、全国各地に同名の神社が存在している。総本社は[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]にある[[宗像大社]]である。
[[航海]]の安全を祈願する神社で、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[近畿地方]]の海沿いの地域に多く存在する。関東地区の[[印旛沼]]周辺には宗像神社が集中する地域がある。
なお、宗像系の神社は、全国で5番目に多いとされている。
== 全国の主な宗像神社 == ===総本社=== * '''[[宗像大社]]'''（[[福岡県]][[宗像市]]） ===北海道地方=== ===東北地方=== *[[青森県]] **[[胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|胸肩神社]]（[[弘前市]]） *[[福島県]] **[[宗像神社 (喜多方市)|宗像神社]]（[[喜多方市]]山都町木曽鎮座） ===関東地方=== *[[埼玉県]] **[[宗像神社 (寄居町)|宗像神社]]（[[寄居町]]） *[[栃木県]] **[[胸形神社 (鹿沼市)|胸形神社]]（[[鹿沼市]]） **[[胸形神社 (小山市)|胸形神社]]（[[小山市]]） **[[網戸神社]]（小山市） *[[千葉県]] **[[宗像神社 (白井市)|宗像神社]]（[[白井市]]） **[[宗像神社 (印西市)|宗像神社]]（[[印西市]]） *[[群馬県]] **[[胸形神社 (高崎市)|胸形神社]]（[[高崎市]]棟高町鎮座）
===東海地方=== *[[静岡県]] **[[宗像神社 (静岡市清水区)|宗像神社]]（[[静岡市]][[清水区]][[興津]]中町554） **[[天宮神社]] (静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]鎮座） **宗像神社（静岡県榛原郡川根本町千頭鎮座） *[[愛知県]] **[[宗像神社 (名古屋市西区)|宗像神社]]（[[名古屋市]]西区浄心鎮座） ===北陸地方=== *[[福井県]] **宗像神社（[[福井県]][[小浜市]]北塩屋鎮座）
===近畿地方=== *[[京都府]] **[[宗像神社 (京都市)|宗像神社]]（京都市上京区、[[京都御苑]]内鎮座） **[[櫟谷宗像神社]]（京都市西京区嵐山鎮座） *[[奈良県]] **[[宗像神社 (桜井市)|宗像神社]]（桜井市外山鎮座） **[[宗像神社 (宇陀市)|宗像神社]]（宇陀市菟田野鎮座）
===中国地方=== *[[岡山県]] **[[宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|宗形神社]]（[[岡山市]]北区大窪） **[[宗形神社 (岡山市南区)|宗形神社]]（[[岡山市]]南区郡） **[[宗形神社 (赤磐市)|宗形神社]]（[[赤磐市]]） *[[鳥取県]] **[[宗形神社 (米子市)|宗形神社]]（[[米子市]]宗像）
===四国地方=== *[[愛媛県]] **[[宗像神社 (新居浜市)|宗像神社]]（[[新居浜市]]） ===九州地方=== *[[福岡県]] **[[宗像神社 (北九州市)|宗像神社]]（[[北九州市]][[小倉南区]]上曽根）

revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The Munakata Three Goddesses—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine === * '''{{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}}''' (Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture)
=== Hokkaido Region ===
=== Tohoku Region === * **Aomori Prefecture** * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City) * **Fukushima Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kitakata)|ja|宗像神社 (喜多方市)}} (Yamato-machi, Kitakata City)

=== Tokai Region === * **Shizuoka Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka)|ja|宗像神社 (静岡市清水区)}} (Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District) * Munakata Shrine (Kawanehon Town, Haibara District) * **Aichi Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Nishi-ku, Nagoya)|ja|宗像神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nishi-ku, Nagoya City)
=== Hokuriku Region === * **Fukui Prefecture** * Munakata Shrine (Obama City, Fukui Prefecture)
=== Kansai Region === * **Kyoto Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * **Nara Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Uda)|ja|宗像神社 (宇陀市)}} (Uda City, Utano)
=== Chugoku Region === * **Okayama Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Minami-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市南区)}} (Minami-ku, Okayama City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|宗形神社 (赤磐市)}} (Akaiwa City) * **Tottori Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Yonago)|ja|宗形神社 (米子市)}} (Yonago City)
=== Shikoku Region === * **Ehime Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Niihama)|ja|宗像神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama City)
=== Kyushu Region === * **Fukuoka Prefecture** * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kitakyushu)|ja|宗像神社 (北九州市)}} (Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu City)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The Munakata Three Goddesses—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Takitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.

revision.text:
'''宗像神社'''（むなかたじんじゃ）は、道主貴である[[宗像三女神]]を祀る[[神社]]。'''宗形神社'''、'''胸形神社'''、'''胸肩神社'''などとも表記される。
== 概要 == 宗像三女神 －田心姫神 ([[タキリビメ]])、湍津姫神 ([[タギツヒメ]])、市杵島姫神 ([[イチキシマヒメ]])－ を祭神としており、全国各地に同名の神社が存在している。総本社は、福岡県宗像市にある[[宗像大社]]である。
航海の安全を祈願する神社で、[[瀬戸内海]]沿岸や[[近畿地方]]の海沿いの地域に多く存在する。関東地区の[[印旛沼]]周辺には、宗像神社が集中する地域がある。
なお、宗像系の神社は、全国で5番目に多いとされている。
== 主な宗像神社 == *[[宗像神社 (喜多方市)|宗像神社]] （[[福島県]][[喜多方市]]山都町） *[[宗像神社 (寄居町)|宗像神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[大里郡]][[寄居町]]藤田） *[[宗像神社 (白井市)|宗像神社]] （[[千葉県]][[白井市]][[清戸 (白井市)|清戸]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市吉高)|宗像神社]] （[[千葉県]][[印西市]][[吉高 (印西市)|吉高]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市船尾)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市[[船尾 (印西市)|船尾]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市戸神)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市[[戸神 (印西市)|戸神]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市岩戸)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市[[岩戸 (印西市)|岩戸]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市吉田)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市[[吉田 (印西市)|吉田]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市鎌苅)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市[[鎌苅]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市師戸)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市[[師戸]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市平賀)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市[[平賀 (印西市)|平賀]]） *[[宗像神社 (印西市瀬戸)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市瀬戸） *[[宗像神社 (印西市山田)|宗像神社]] （千葉県印西市山田） *[[宗像神社 (さいたま市大宮区)|宗像神社]] （[[埼玉県]][[さいたま市]][[大宮区]]高鼻町） – [[氷川神社]]摂社 *[[宗像神社 (寄居町)|宗像神社]] （埼玉県[[大里郡]][[寄居町]]藤田） *[[宗像神社 (中野区)|宗像神社]] （[[東京都]][[中野区]][[弥生町 (中野区)|弥生町]]） – [[神明氷川神社]]末社 *[[宗像神社 (小浜市)|宗像神社]] （[[福井県]][[小浜市]]北塩屋） *[[宗像神社 (静岡市清水区)|宗像神社]] （[[静岡県]][[静岡市]][[清水区]][[興津]]中町） *[[宗像神社 (名古屋市西区)|宗像神社]] （[[愛知県]][[名古屋市]][[西区 (名古屋市)|西区]][[上名古屋]]） *[[宗像神社 (京都市)|宗像神社]] （[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]]京都御苑） – [[京都御苑]]内 *[[櫟谷宗像神社]] （京都府京都市[[西京区]]嵐山中尾下町） *[[宗像神社 (桜井市)|宗像神社]] （[[奈良県]][[桜井市]]外山） – [[鳥見山]]山麓 *[[宗像神社 (宇陀市)|宗像神社]] （奈良県[[宇陀市]]菟田野） *[[宗像神社 (観音寺市)|宗像神社]] （[[香川県]][[観音寺市]]大野原町） *[[宗像神社 (新居浜市)|宗像神社]] （[[愛媛県]][[新居浜市]]八雲町） *[[宗像神社 (北九州市)|宗像神社]] （[[福岡県]][[北九州市]][[小倉南区]]上曽根） *'''[[宗像大社]]''' （福岡県[[宗像市]]田島） - 総本社
== 主な宗形神社 ==
* [[宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|宗形神社]] （[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[北区 (岡山市)|北区]]大窪） * [[宗形神社 (岡山市南区)|宗形神社]] （岡山県[[岡山市]][[南区 (岡山市)|南区]]郡） * [[宗形神社 (赤磐市)|宗形神社]] （岡山県[[赤磐市]]是里） * [[宗形神社 (米子市)|宗形神社]] （鳥取県[[米子市]]宗像）
== 主な胸形神社 ==
* [[胸形神社 (鹿沼市)|胸形神社]] （[[栃木県]][[鹿沼市]]村井町） * [[胸形神社 (小山市)|胸形神社]] （栃木県[[小山市]]寒川） * [[胸形神社 (高崎市)|胸形神社]] （[[群馬県]][[高崎市]]棟高町） * [[胸形神社 (静岡市葵区)|胸形神社]] （静岡県静岡市[[葵区]]瀬名） * [[胸形神社 (豊田市)|胸形神社]] （愛知県[[豊田市]][[八草町]]）
== 主な胸肩神社 ==
* [[胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|胸肩神社]] （[[青森県]][[弘前市]]品川町）
== 他の宗像三女神を祀る神社 ==
* [[網戸神社]] （栃木県小山市網戸） * [[天宮神社]] （静岡県[[周智郡]][[森町 (静岡県)|森町]]天宮）
== 関連項目 == *[[宗像三女神]] *[[宗像大社]] *[[厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)|厳島神社]] *[[市杵島神社]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:むなかたしんしや}} [[Category:宗像神社|!]]

revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine === * '''{{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}}''' (Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture)
=== Hokkaido Region ===
=== Tohoku Region === * '''Aomori Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City) * '''Fukushima Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kitakata)|ja|宗像神社 (喜多方市)}} (Yamato-machi, Kitakata City)
=== Kanto Region === * '''Saitama Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Yorii)|ja|宗像神社 (寄居町)}} (Yorii Town) * '''Tochigi Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Kanuma)|ja|胸形神社 (鹿沼市)}} (Kanuma City) ** {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) ** {{ill|Amido Shrine|ja|網戸神社}} (Oyama City) * '''Chiba Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Inzai)|ja|宗像神社 (印西市)}} (Inzai City) * '''Gunma Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Takasaki)|ja|胸形神社 (高崎市)}} (Takasaki City, Muneoka Town)
=== Tokai Region === * '''Shizuoka Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka)|ja|宗像神社 (静岡市清水区)}} (Shimizu-ku, Shizuoka City) ** {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District) ** Munakata Shrine (Kawanehon Town, Haibara District) * '''Aichi Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Nishi-ku, Nagoya)|ja|宗像神社 (名古屋市西区)}} (Nishi-ku, Nagoya City)
=== Hokuriku Region === * '''Fukui Prefecture''' ** Munakata Shrine (Obama City, Fukui Prefecture)
=== Kansai Region === * '''Kyoto Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) ** {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * '''Nara Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City) ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Uda)|ja|宗像神社 (宇陀市)}} (Uda City, Utano)
=== Chugoku Region === * '''Okayama Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Minami-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市南区)}} (Minami-ku, Okayama City) ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Akaiwa)|ja|宗形神社 (赤磐市)}} (Akaiwa City) * '''Tottori Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Yonago)|ja|宗形神社 (米子市)}} (Yonago City)
=== Shikoku Region === * '''Ehime Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Niihama)|ja|宗像神社 (新居浜市)}} (Niihama City)
=== Kyushu Region === * '''Fukuoka Prefecture''' ** {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kitakyushu)|ja|宗像神社 (北九州市)}} (Kokuraminami-ku, Kitakyushu City)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.

revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)

* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Completion tier 5]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)
== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City)== See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社}}

revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|12=draft}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=draft}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=draft}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|12=draft}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|12=draft}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)|12=draft}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine|12=draft}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|12=draft}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社|12=draft}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)|12=draft}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|12=draft}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社|12=draft}}

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Taisha|qq=}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|qq=}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|qq=}} (Oyama City) *

'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|qq=}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|qq=}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|qq=}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomiya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichitanidani Munakata Shrine|qq=}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|qq=}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11451876]] Wikidata name: Munakata shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗像神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11451876]] Wikidata name: Munakata shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|qq=}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|qq=}} (Oyama City) *

* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichitani Munakata Shrine|qq=}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|qq=}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗像神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11451876]] Wikidata name: Munakata shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.

The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗像神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗像神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:宗像神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11451876]] Wikidata name: Munakata shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|qq=}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|qq=}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|qq=}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomiya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichitani Munakata Shrine|qq=}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|qq=}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|qq=}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Itsukushima Shrines (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11410137}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima Shrine|qq=}}

Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|qq=}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|qq=}} (Oyama City) *
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|ja|宗像神社 (白井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Shiroi)|qq=}} (Shiroi City) * {{ill|Amanomiya Shrine|ja|天宮神社|1=Ame-no-Miya Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanomiya Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Miya Shrine|qq=}} (Morimachi, Shuchi District)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichitani Munakata Shrine|qq=}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|qq=}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)

revision.text:
'''Munakata Shrine''' (宗像神社, Munakata Jinja) is a shrine dedicated to the Munakata Three Goddesses (宗像三女神), who are deities of the sea and navigation. The shrine is also referred to as '''Munakata Shrine''' (宗形神社), '''Munekata Shrine''' (胸形神社), and '''Munakata Shrine''' (胸肩神社), among other variations.
== Overview ==
The {{ill|Munakata Three Goddesses|ja|宗像三女神|1=Three Munakata Goddesses|lt=Munakata Three Goddesses|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Three Goddesses|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Munakata Goddesses|qq=}}—{{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|湍津姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}}, and {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}—are enshrined as the main deities. Shrines bearing the name "Munakata Shrine" are found across Japan, with the head shrine being {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} in Munakata City, Fukuoka Prefecture.
Munakata Shrines are primarily dedicated to ensuring safe navigation and are concentrated along coastal areas of the Seto Inland Sea and the Kansai region. In the Kanto region, a cluster of Munakata Shrines can be found around {{ill|Inbanuma|ja|印旛沼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inbanuma|qq=}}. Notably, Munakata-lineage shrines Rank fifth in number nationwide.
== Major Munakata Shrines Nationwide ==
=== Head Shrine ===
*
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|ja|胸肩神社 (青森県弘前市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Hirosaki)|qq=}} (Hirosaki City)
* * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munakata Shrine (Oyama)|lt=Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|ja|胸形神社 (小山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munekata Shrine (Oyama)|qq=}} (Oyama City) *
*

* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|宗像神社 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} (Kyoto Gyoen, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Ichitani Munakata Shrine|ja|櫟谷宗像神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichitani Munakata Shrine|qq=}} (Arashiyama, Kyoto City) * {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|ja|宗像神社 (桜井市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Sakurai)|qq=}} (Toyama, Sakurai City)
* {{ill|Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|ja|宗形神社 (岡山市北区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Munakata Shrine (Kita-ku, Okayama)|qq=}} (Kita-ku, Okayama City) == See Also == * {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|qid=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} * {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|ja|厳島神社 (曖昧さ回避)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine (disambiguation)|1=Itsukushima Shrines (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11410137}} * {{ill|Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrines|lt=Ichikishima Shrine|ja|市杵島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima Shrine|qq=}}
